title: Peppery Tender Greek Lamb Stew
description: A cozy, low-sodium Greek lamb stew infused with dry sherry and a subtle pepper kick, finished with a splash of lemon.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the cubed lamb and sear on all sides until browned, about 5 minutes. Remove the lamb and set aside.
In the same pot, add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, red pepper flakes, and dried oregano; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return the lamb to the pot and pour in the dry sherry, scraping up any brown bits from the bottom. Let the sherry reduce for 2 minutes.
Add the canned tomatoes and low-sodium chicken broth. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low and cover. Cook for 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
After the hour, stir in the Greek yogurt, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Simmer uncovered for an additional 15 minutes so the flavors meld.
Season with black pepper, adjust seasoning if needed, and serve hot over a bed of steamed rice or crusty bread.
Remove the pot from heat and let the stew rest for 5 minutes before ladling into bowls. Enjoy the tender lamb and peppery warmth of this traditional Greek favorite!
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
